step_by_step_reasoning: We start by identifying the physical icons on the whiteboard. The users are represented by a User icon ('USERS'), which corresponds to UserConsumerMobile. They upload a digital 'CREDENTIAL' (screenshot, PDF, or photo), mapped as ThirdParty. This credential request goes to the Elastic Load Balancer ('ELB'). The ELB is integrated with 'WAF' and 'Shield Advanced' for security checks and DDoS mitigation. The backend microservices run on Amazon 'EKS' and scale using 'AUTOSCALING'. 'Terraform' is drawn on the board as the IaC tool used for deployment but is not actively connected via arrows, so it remains a standalone node.

graph.name: Matter: Verifier API for Digital Identity
graph.notes: Matter built a Verifier API to pre-verify digital credentials (like the New Zealand My Vaccine Pass) before events. The architecture scales automatically to handle hundreds of thousands of requests using EKS and AWS Shield Advanced.

nodes.name: Users
nodes.notes: Fitness club members or event attendees uploading their verification credentials.

nodes.name: Credential
nodes.notes: My Vaccine Pass credentials in the form of a screenshot, PDF, or photo.

nodes.name: ELB
nodes.notes: Elastic Load Balancer routing incoming verification requests.

nodes.name: WAF
nodes.notes: Performs sanity checks, drops unauthorized headers, and validates content length.

nodes.name: Shield Advanced
nodes.notes: Protects infrastructure against DDoS attacks during high-traffic launch events.

nodes.name: EKS
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Runs the core Verifier API microservices.

nodes.name: Auto Scaling
nodes.notes: Automatically scales microservices out and in based on traffic peak requirements.

nodes.name: Terraform
nodes.notes: Infrastructure as Code tool used to consistently replicate and deploy environments.

edges.notes: User provides their digital vaccine credential.
Credential photo or document is sent to the Verifier API endpoint.
ELB passes traffic requests to AWS WAF for header inspection.
Shield Advanced ensures incoming peaks are not flagged as DDoS attacks.
Clean traffic is forwarded to microservices running on EKS.
Auto Scaling manages the EKS worker nodes to handle load changes.
